steps.title: Identify the key difference to emphasize
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference, not similarities. From the notes, the critical distinction is their different ingredient requirements: baking soda needs to be mixed with a separate acidic ingredient to release carbon dioxide, while baking powder already contains the acid mixed in.

steps.title: Find the choice presenting this contrast
steps.content: Choice D directly states this difference using “whereas”—baking soda requires an acidic ingredient, but baking powder does not. Choices A and B describe both as leavening agents producing CO2, treating them as equivalent; choice C focuses on unrelated facts rather than contrasting the two. That matches choice D — D is correct.
